decisions.title_en: Nvidia Q1 FY27 actuals: $81.6B revenue (+85% YoY), data center $75.2B (+92%), Q2 guide $91B beats whisper $90B, $80B buyback added, dividend up to $0.25, China DC still zero
decisions.reason: Previously published 2026-05-20 (preview piece on expectations: $78B revenue, $73B DC, China zero assumption, Q2 guide >Q1 print). New info: actual Q1 print $81.6B beat (+$3.6B vs preview consensus), data center $75.2B (+92% YoY, $2B above preview), Q2 guide $91B (vs $87B consensus / $90B whisper), $80B buyback authorized, dividend raised to $0.25, sovereign AI tripled to $30B FY26 (~14% revenue), $500B Blackwell+Rubin backlog disclosed. Stock paradoxically slid in after-hours despite the beat. Title/summary will lead with the actual print numbers and Q2 guide vs preview expectations.

decisions.title_en: SpaceX files public S-1 for record IPO: targets $75B raise at $1.75-2T valuation, first audited financials show xAI burned $6.4B in 2025, Anthropic compute deal $1.25B/month through 2029
decisions.reason: story_key not in lookback. Only related lookback entry is `spacexai-staff-exodus` (5-14, talent exodus), which is a different story. This is the actual public S-1 filing for the IPO itself — first audited income statement, cash flow, risk factors of the merged SpaceX-xAI entity. Massive new info: Starlink $11.4B revenue / $4.4B operating income 2025; xAI $6.4B 2025 loss on $3.2B revenue; combined capex jumped 5x to $20.7B (2025); $12.7B went to AI; Anthropic locks $1.25B/month for Colossus + Colossus II through May 2029, gets 300+ MW capacity; targets June 12 first trading day.

decisions.title_en: Axios scoop: Trump AI EO draft splits into cybersecurity + 'covered frontier models' track requiring voluntary 90-day pre-release government access; could publish as early as this week
decisions.reason: Previously published 2026-05-13 (Axios original: Wiles + Sacks publicly walked back Hassett 'FDA for AI' EO, said to wait for Beijing summit). New info on 5-20: actual EO draft text now circulated — split into TWO sections: (1) cybersecurity for Pentagon/critical infrastructure/threat-sharing, (2) 'covered frontier models' with voluntary framework requiring labs to share models with government 90 days pre-release plus critical infrastructure provider access. Publishing window: 'as early as this week'. This is the post-walkback EO actually materializing — substantively new content vs the walkback narrative of 5-13.

decisions.title_en: Google opens WebMCP origin trial in Chrome 149 — first browser-native AI-agent web standard ships from prototype to production traffic
decisions.reason: story_key not in lookback. Lookback for 5-19 covers `google-antigravity-2-launch` (Antigravity 2.0, Managed Agents API) and `gemini-omni-leak-io-2026` (Gemini Omni Flash launch) but NOT WebMCP. This is a separate substantial I/O announcement: WebMCP — proposed W3C open standard — exits behind-a-flag prototype and enters public origin trial in Chrome 149 starting 5-19; navigator.modelContext API; Microsoft Edge 147 already shipped support in March. Browser-native AI agent web is now testable on production traffic.

decisions.title_en: Apple publishes WWDC 2026 schedule; confirms iOS 27 'Extensions' framework will open Siri to Claude / Gemini / Grok / Mistral / Perplexity, ending OpenAI exclusive
decisions.reason: story_key not in lookback. Apple released WWDC 2026 schedule on 5-19 (keynote June 8, conference through June 12) with confirmed slate showcasing iOS 27, Siri 2.0 overhaul, and the 'Extensions' framework that lets users pick Gemini / Claude / Grok / Mistral / Perplexity as their preferred AI provider in Settings — ending OpenAI's exclusive arrangement. This is the schedule/confirmation event after months of rumors; first time Apple itself has publicly committed to a multi-provider Siri.

decisions.title_en: Armada raises $230M Series B at $2B valuation for modular AI data centers; Johnson Controls partners on 400k sq ft Arizona 'Galleon Forge One' factory producing Leviathan units for military/oil/mining/remote sites
decisions.reason: story_key not in lookback. Announced 5-19: $230M Series B, $2B post-money, co-led by Overmatch + 8090 Industries + BlackRock (new). Manufacturing JV with Johnson Controls — 400,000 sq ft Arizona factory ('Galleon Forge One', 500+ jobs, starts producing 'Leviathan' megawatt-scale modular data centers this summer). Customer base: military operations, oil fields, mining sites, remote industrial — the 'edge AI compute' frontier vs. hyperscale data centers.

decisions.title_en: Google adds $100 AI Ultra entry tier and cuts top Ultra from $250 to $200, plus AI Pro gets YouTube Premium Lite
decisions.reason: story_key already published 2026-05-19 ('Google Antigravity 2.0 …—AI Ultra 反向降价 $250→$200'). The $250→$200 cut and Antigravity/Managed Agents were the headline. The new $100 tier and YouTube Premium Lite bundling are minor pricing additions to the same already-covered subscription reshuffle; no substantively new development warranting a second card.

decisions.title_en: Code with Claude London runs May 20-21, plus 'Extended London' day for indie devs/founders
decisions.reason: London tour stop is a re-airing of the San Francisco 5-6 keynote (Dreaming, Outcomes, Multi-Agent Orchestration, Claude Code routines, Advisor, Claude for Small Business, Claude Design). No new product or model unveiled at the London event itself.
